text:
<think>I should start by stating the initial number of pens Ram buys, which is 25.</think>
Ram starts with 25 pens.
<think>Next, I should explain that after using these pens, he will have 25 empty pens, which he can use to make new pens by mixing ink from five empties.</think>
After using all 25 pens, he will have 25 empty pens.
<think>Now, I need to calculate how many new pens he can make from these 25 empty pens, knowing that 5 empties make 1 new pen.</think>
He can make 25 ÷ 5 = 5 new pens from the empties.
<think>I should mention that after using these 5 new pens, he will again have 5 empty pens.</think>
After using the 5 new pens, he will have 5 more empty pens.
<think>Now, I should note that these 5 empties can be combined to make one more pen.</think>
He can mix the ink from these 5 empties to make 1 more pen.
<think>After using this last pen, he will have 1 empty pen left, which is not enough to make another full pen.</think>
After using this final pen, he will have 1 empty pen left, which is not enough to make another full pen.
<think>Now, I should sum up all the pens he got to use: the original 25, the 5 made from empties, and the 1 made from those empties.</think>
So, the total number of pens Ram gets to use is 25 + 5 + 1 = 31.
<think>I should now present the final answer after the #### as requested.</think>
#### 31
